title: useAsyncOutput
description: Creates a resource with persisted outputs. The primary hook for CReact components that interact with external systems.
signature: useAsyncOutput<O, P>(propsOrGetter: P | (() => P), handler: Handler<P, O>): OutputAccessors<O>
param_props_or_getter_desc: Static props or a getter function for reactive tracking. When a getter is used, the handler re-runs when dependencies change.
param_handler_type: <0>(props: P, setOutputs: SetOutputs<O>) => Promise<(() => void) | void> | void</0>
param_handler_desc: Setup function that performs work and sets outputs. May return a cleanup function (sync or async).
returns_desc: An object with accessor functions for each output key. For example, if you call <0>setOutputs({ url: '...' })</0>, the return has <1>result.url()</1>.
usage_counter: The durable counter's <0>Counter</0> calls <1>useAsyncOutput</1> once with empty props and a handler. The returned <2>counter.count()</2> is a reactive accessor that any effect can read.
usage_restore: The page writer's handler reads <0>prev</0> inside <1>setOutputs</1> to detect an already-generated page. When <2>state === "ready"</2> was restored, it skips the Claude call and returns early instead of regenerating.
usage_prev_aware: The site publisher compares the incoming <0>entry.hash</0> against the persisted <1>prev.hash</1>. Matching hashes mean the object is already uploaded, so the handler returns before touching <2>S3</2>.
callout_idempotent: Handlers must be idempotent. On restart, handlers re-run for all nodes to re-establish side effects (intervals, subscriptions, etc.). Output values are restored from state, but handlers must re-create any runtime artifacts.
in_the_wild: Watch these handlers persist and recover across restarts in the <0>durable-counter walkthrough</0>.
